University of Maryland Student Academic Success-Degree Completion Policy

Change of Major

To process a change of major, please visit the college associated with the new major for approval and advising. The change can generally be processed by an advisor.

University of Maryland policy stipulates that full-time degree seeking students are expected to complete their undergraduate degree program in four years.

Some majors have special admission requirements. See lep.umd.edu for information on Limited Enrollment Programs at the University of Maryland.

To meet this expectation, students must plan carefully in consultation with an academic advisor; complete 30 credits each year (usually accomplished through a course load of 14-16 credits per semester); satisfy general education, prerequisite and other course requirements with acceptable grades in a timely manner; and meet the benchmarks.

A student must be enrolled in the major and minor programs from which he or she plans to graduate when registering for the final 15 hours of the baccalaureate program.

Academic units provide benchmarks and sample templates of multi-semester plans leading to four-year graduation. Students are required to map out individualized four-year plans consistent with these guidelines and benchmarks, and are responsible for updating them as circumstances change. Students who do not meet benchmarks are required to select a more suitable major.

Second Major: A student wishing to complete a second major in addition to their primary major, must obtain written permission from both the college dean for the primary major and the college dean for the secondary major.
